questions.question: Which statement best describes Inflammation?
questions.option_c: Protective response involving vascular and cellular events
questions.option_d: Inability of heart to pump sufficient blood to meet demands
questions.explanation: Acute inflammation: neutrophils; chronic: macrophages/lymphocytes.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Edema?
questions.option_b: Progressive loss of kidney function over months/years
questions.explanation: Caused by increased hydrostatic pressure, decreased oncotic pressure, etc.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Heart failure?
questions.option_a: Inability of heart to pump sufficient blood to meet demands
questions.option_c: Systemic hypoperfusion leading to cellular injury
questions.explanation: Leads to congestion; neurohormonal activation (RAAS/SNS).

questions.question: Which statement best describes COPD?
questions.option_b: Progressive loss of kidney function over months/years
questions.option_c: Protective response involving vascular and cellular events
questions.explanation: Often due to smoking; decreased FEV1/FVC.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Diabetes mellitus?
questions.option_b: Chronic inflammatory disease of arteries with plaque formation
questions.explanation: Chronic complications include micro- and macrovascular disease.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Hypertension?
questions.option_d: Inability of heart to pump sufficient blood to meet demands
questions.explanation: Risk factor for stroke, MI, CKD.

questions.question: Which statement best describes CKD?
questions.option_a: Inability of heart to pump sufficient blood to meet demands
questions.option_d: Progressive loss of kidney function over months/years
questions.explanation: Leads to anemia, mineral-bone disorder, uremia.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Shock?
questions.option_a: Chronic inflammatory disease of arteries with plaque formation
questions.option_c: Systemic hypoperfusion leading to cellular injury
questions.explanation: Types: hypovolemic, cardiogenic, distributive, obstructive.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Thrombosis?
questions.option_a: Progressive loss of kidney function over months/years
questions.option_d: Protective response involving vascular and cellular events
questions.explanation: Virchow triad: stasis, endothelial injury, hypercoagulability.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Atherosclerosis?
questions.option_b: Systemic hypoperfusion leading to cellular injury
questions.option_c: Chronic inflammatory disease of arteries with plaque formation
questions.explanation: LDL oxidation and macrophage foam cells are key features.
